page: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of a 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, and AES cryptography.

This instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external persistent disks. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with the instance, and the server does not feature any GPU hardware.

In benchmark testing, the E8-4s_v4 exhibits average single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores. However, its multi-core CPU performance falls into the lower tier, with weak results in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached and large uncached blocks also register in the bottom 25 percent, though the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating. This profile indicates a resource-density tradeoff, where the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense multi-threaded compute power.

Given its high memory capacity relative to its 4 vCPUs, the E8-4s_v4 is suited for memory-intensive workloads that require a large working set but moderate processing power. Suitable applications include small to medium relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications with high memory footprints but low parallel computational demands.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides dedicated CPU allocation across 2 physical cores. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU and Passmark memory performance, contrasted with weaker multi-core compute and memory bandwidth results. This hardware profile represents a qualitative cost-efficiency tradeoff, prioritizing high memory density over raw parallel processing power. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate CPU demands, such as in-memory caching, small databases, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
